issue_title: STDIN prompts rendering is nondeterministic with respect to stream outputs
issue_body:
I believe the Jupyter FE is receiving STDIN and stream messages in a nondeterministic order.

Here's a small repro:

```python
import getpass, sys
print (2)
sys.stdout.flush()
_ = getpass.getpass()
```

I would expect this to render 2 before the getpass prompt in all executions. Yet, if I execute this a few times, I occasionally see the reverse order. (Perhaps 1-2 instances in 10 executions.) 

Example screenshot:
![screenshot2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/151865/34274789-15792b68-e64f-11e7-8a17-1957542db15f.png)

My conjecture is that what is happening is that STDIN channel messages and IOPUB messages are not deterministically ordered for a given cell execution.

issue_title: Don't use column names/labels as CSS classes
issue_body:
Nick and I spent almost an hour tracking this one down: One of my grid columns was coming from our `LookupMixin` which has a column named `label`. I was showing that label on webgrid and you could filter it. But webgrid used the column name `label` as the class on the filter HTML. Bootstrap *also* has a `label` class. So you can imagine the styling was all wrong.

Basically this was an extremely surprising bug and we really should not be doing this. We should derive some highly unlikely name for our CSS classes instead of copying directly from the column names.

issue_title: tags get added when tokenizing with bleach 2.0
issue_body:
When I redid Bleach to support html5lib >= 0.99999999 (8 9s), I rewrote the sanitizing code to operate as an html5lib filter rather than as a tokenizer. The result of this is that the tokenizer cleans up the HTML before Bleach gets a chance to work on it and the act of cleaning up the HTML sometimes changes or creates new tags.

I wrote up this issue: https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python/issues/312

For example, if you had:
```
import bleach
bleach.clean('Yeah, sure. <sarcasm/>', strip=False)
```
it'd get turned into:
```
'Yeah, sure. <sarcasm></sarcasm>.'
```
and then that'd get escaped/dropped/whatever.

Adding this as an issue to help catch other regressions and use cases so I can figure out if I want to spend more time looking into a fix.

issue_title: Consistent Async ActionType Constants
issue_body:
We should pick a convention for naming action type strings, actions, and action types 😸.

Rationale:

1. It's really nice to be able to drop into any package/app directory and have a sense of what things are doing by looking at actions.
2. It's really nice to be able to look at redux devtools and see consistent patterns as action types are logging out.
3. It'd be nice to not have to think about it when we're clicking in these boiler plate defs.

## Proposal

Let's try to standardize three things (yes, this is boiler plate-y).

### Actual type strings

How about we standardize on the following? I feel like this is a pretty common approach and I would find it helpful to have direction on this one?

```
[<PREFIX>/, ..]<VERB>_<NOUN>
[<PREFIX>/, ..]<VERB>_<NOUN>_FULFILLED
[<PREFIX>/, ..]<VERB>_<NOUN>_FAILED
[<PREFIX>/, ..]<VERB>_<NOUN>_CANCELED
```

Example:

```
CORE/FETCH_HOST
CORE/FETCH_HOST_FULFILLED
CORE/FETCH_HOST_FAILED
CORE/FETCH_HOST_CANCELED
```

### Type constants

This one's simple, and we do this already. Just use all but the prefix.

`const <VERB>_<NOUN> = "[<PREFIX>/, ..]<VERB>_<NOUN>";`

`const FETCH_HOST = "CORE/FETCH_HOST";`

### Action creator

Also simple, and we do this already. Just `camelCase` the type constant.

`const <verb><Noun> = () => { .. };`
`const fetchHost = () => { .. };`

### Action creator type

Also also simple, and we mostly do this. Just `PascalCase` the action creator name.

`type <Verb><Noun> = { .. };`
`const FetchHost = { .. };`


### Example

```
const FETCH_HOST = "CORE/FETCH_HOST";
type FetchHost = { .. };
const fetchHost = ({ .. }) => { .. };
```

## Examples from the codebase

### bare + postfixes

```
<VERB>_<NOUN>
<VERB>_<NOUN>_FULFILLED
<VERB>_<NOUN>_FAILED
<VERB>_<NOUN>_CANCELED
```

This sorta breaks down when you don't have a noun... `fetchInterrupt

e.g.,

```
FETCH_KERNELSPECS
FETCH_KERNELSPECS_FULFILLED
FETCH_KERNELSPECS_FAILED
```

e.g.,

```
LOAD
LOAD_FAILED
```

### bare + prefixes

```
<VERB>
DONE_<VERB>
```

e.g.,

```
SAVE_CONFIG 
DONE_SAVING_CONFIG
```

issue_title: SP with global inhibition should always obey numActiveColumnsPerInhArea
issue_body:
See discussion: https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/pull/1164#discussion_r90347310

With local inhibition, the `inhibitionRadius` varies with the statistics of the input, so if you're using the `numActiveColumnsPerInhArea` parameter, for certain inputs you might end up with way more active columns than you want, because the inhibition areas might be small. So it's probably better to use the `localAreaDensity` when using local inhibition. Anyway, the code does some work to minimize this effect: it makes sure to use a density no greater than 0.5. https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/fbd7ac40a23a536317c76b85089e90f5bc5f1fe2/src/nupic/research/spatial_pooler.py#L1400 

This logic also happens in the boosting code.

So this check is addressing the problem that `numActiveColumnsPerInhArea` is awkward with local inhibition. We should move this check into the local inhibition code path. The global inhibition path should simply obey the parameters, since the `inhibitionRadius` is predictable and doesn't change with the statistics of the input.

issue_title: Excel for Mac Date Offset
issue_body:
Hello, first of all, thanks for this great tool.

I'm facing an issue with date formatting: I have some cells containing '6-15', which was intended to represent an integer range, but Excel converts it to date, and when I read the file I get a datetime. The sheets I'm trying to import can't be modified, so I'm guessing if there's a way to force the reference date for reading, or to get the reference used in the book so I can correct the date after parsing it.

BTW, I think this is exactly the same problem as #54 . I've tested it in my computer, switching from one reference date to another, and the dates match.

issue_title: Consistency with Sorting
issue_body:
**Problem:**
```
    train_iter, dev_iter, test_iter = data.BucketIterator.splits(
        (train, dev, test),
        batch_sizes=(32, 256, 256),
        sort_key=lambda x: len(x.input),
        sort=True,
        device=-1)  # Use CPU
```

If `sort=True` and `train=True`, then the `train_iter` batches are shuffled. This behavior is unexpected.

**Cause:**
Because by default `self.shuffle=True` is `train=True`. Then https://github.com/pytorch/text/blob/master/torchtext/data/iterator.py#L113 `shuffle` overrides `sort`.

In addition, the BucketIterator https://github.com/pytorch/text/blob/master/torchtext/data/iterator.py#L241 assumes that the data is sorted; therefore, skipping `pool` that would have sorted https://github.com/pytorch/text/blob/master/torchtext/data/iterator.py#L241. 

**Possible Solution:**
`sort=True` should override `shuffle=None and train=True`.

issue_title: transforms.Lambda fails on class functions
issue_body:
`transforms.Lambda` appears to fail when a function that is passed to it is a class function:

```python
import torchvision

class MyClass(object):

    def my_transform(self, img):
        return img

def my_transform(img):
    return img

t = MyClass()

print('Pure function works')
torchvision.transforms.Lambda(my_transform)
print('Class function fails')
torchvision.transforms.Lambda(t.my_transform)
```
Output:
```
Pure function works
Class function fails
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test_pytorch.py", line 17, in <module>
    torchvision.transforms.Lambda(t.my_transform)
  File "/braintree/home/qbilius/libs/miniconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torchvision/transforms/transforms.py", line 279, in __init__
    assert isinstance(lambd, types.LambdaType)
AssertionError
```

My understanding it that `isinstance(lambd, types.LambdaType)` is not the best way to check if something is a function or not. Rather, `callable` or `__call__` [should be used](https://stackoverflow.com/a/624939).

I could submit a quick PR if that seems like a good solution.

issue_title: account.billing_info raises AttributeError on new accounts.
issue_body:
Repeat of #98, but I'm having this occur in 2.2.17. I should note I'm on python 3.5.0, just in case.

Using the example in the docs for creating a new account, and then a new billing info object:

```py
account = Account(account_code='1')
account.email = 'verena@example.com'
account.first_name = 'Verena'
account.last_name = 'Example'
account.save()

# account.billing_info here raises AttributeError, 
# so I copy-paste the example code exactly, including the get.

account = Account.get('1')
billing_info = account.billing_info #  but this still raises an AttributeError!
billing_info.token_id = 'TOKEN_ID'
billing_info.save()
```

I seem to have been able to resolve this by calling `account.update_billing_info` with a new BillingInfo object I create and add the token to, like so:

```py
account = Account.get('1')
billing_info = BillingInfo()
billing_info.token_id = 'TOKEN_ID'
account.update_billing_info(billing_info)
```

Before I arrived to the solution above, I also tried attaching the billing info object I created to the account and saving it, but that raised an unrelated AttributeError, so I didn't continue down that path. 

While I have a solution that works for me as-is, I'm curious as to whether this is intended to be used this way, or if I'm going against the grain, since the example differed from my implementation. 

Thanks for your time!

issue_title: Subscription add-ons' unit amounts should use the Subscription's currency
issue_body:
In an API response with a Subscription with a `subscription_add_ons` member, the add-ons' `unit_amount_in_cents` are modeled as `Money` instances. However, the matching `currency` for those Money instances is on the Subscription, not the add-on, so something goes awry and they can't be used.

The add-ons should fall back to using the Subscription's `currency` so the amounts are still available.

issue_title: Use enum.Enum instead of integer constants?
issue_body:
Most of the objects we use have a number of integer constants that are really enums. Python 3.4 has support for enums, so should we use it?

issue_title: Angular distance function still calculating the euclidean distance? (annoylib.h)
Memory Leakage
issue_body:
Hi there,

annoy (great library!) is our goto solution for NN search. I'm creating multiple annoy indices, add multiple samples to each, save each, and unload each:
`
import memory
from annoy import AnnoyIndex
import numpy as np

dim = 49152
nsamples = 209

def vectors_to_add():
    v = nsamples * [None]
    for i in range(len(v)):
        v[i] = np.zeros(dim)
    return v

def build_index(count):
    knn = AnnoyIndex(dim, 'euclidean')
    v = vectors_to_add()
    for i,vector in enumerate(v):
        knn.add_item(i,vector)
    knn.save('knn-%d.aix' %count)
    knn.unload()
    del knn # to make sure (?)

for count in range(100):
    build_index(count)
    print 'Added index no.', count, ': total memory -> ', memory.memory()/(1024**3), 'GB'
`

When running on a (virtualized) Debian 9, I experience massive memory leakage:
Added index no. 0 : total memory ->  0.459384918213 GB
Added index no. 1 : total memory ->  0.769920349121 GB
Added index no. 2 : total memory ->  1.003074646 GB
Added index no. 3 : total memory ->  1.2364730835 GB
Added index no. 4 : total memory ->  1.46962738037 GB
...

When running on an Ubuntu 16.04, things are fine:
Added index no. 0 : total memory ->  0.232261657715 GB
Added index no. 1 : total memory ->  0.231666564941 GB
Added index no. 2 : total memory ->  0.231666564941 GB
Added index no. 3 : total memory ->  0.231666564941 GB
Added index no. 4 : total memory ->  0.231666564941 GB
Added index no. 5 : total memory ->  0.231666564941 GB
...

Remarks:
- I'm not even running build() on the index structure, just adding samples.
- I looked at the C-code in annoylib.h and cannot find any leak.
- I wonder if memory mapping (mmap/munmap) is the issue (?) or anything with the C-Python-interface (?)
- The memory measurement (memory.py) is self-implemented and drawn from [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/938733/total-memory-used-by-python-process) (reads from /proc/pid/status, should be OK, I can share if it helps).
- I tried with annoy installed from pip (1.9.3) and from source (same issue).

My questions:
1. Is there any hint to what's going on? (I'm aware this may be rather a platform issue than an issue with annoy itself. Still, any pointers are greatly appreciated ;-) 
2. What system libraries can I check/update?
3. Should I check if MAP_POPULATE (annoylib.h) is set? (and if yes, how do I do that?)

Kind regards (and thank you so much!),
Adrian

issue_title: Subtask: Sports scores + stats
Awkward to set context_codes[] parameter in Calendar.list_calendar_events call
issue_body:
The `Canvas.list_calendar_events` method feeds its parameters down to a `GET /api/v1/calendar_events` request. [That request](https://canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/calendar_events.html#method.calendar_events_api.index) accepts a `context_codes[]` parameter, but setting that parameter correctly is surprisingly awkward in version 0.6.0 of `canvasapi`. The problem is that the parameter name really must be `context_codes[]`, not `context_codes`, but creating a Python keyword argument with brackets in its name is subtle:

```python
context = 'course_123456'

canvas.list_calendar_events(context_codes=context) 
# canvasapi.exceptions.CanvasException: API encountered an error processing your request

canvas.list_calendar_events(context_codes=[context])
# canvasapi.exceptions.CanvasException: API encountered an error processing your request

canvas.list_calendar_events(context_codes[]=context)
# SyntaxError: invalid syntax

canvas.list_calendar_events('context_codes[]'=context)
# SyntaxError: invalid syntax

canvas.list_calendar_events(**{'context_codes[]': context})
# success!
```

The syntactically invalid ones I knew were wrong before I even tried them. But for the first two attempts shown above, I guess I expected `canvasapi` to add the brackets for me either because it knew that `context_codes[]` was special, or perhaps it would always append brackets when I passed a value as a list (`[context]`) rather than a single item.

Arguably this is my fault: give the wrong parameter name, and of course things will fail. But I would have hoped that `canvasapi` would make this easier. The use of `**{...}` is obscure enough that many `canvasapi` users may not know how to do it at all.

issue_title: Ignore JSON content type if request is HTTP GET
issue_body:
A JSON body on a GET request has no contextual meaning, but is technically allowed. In doctor if you hit a GET handler and have content-type set to application/json it currently causes a 500 error which gets re-raised by doctor.

We should instead ignore and not try to load the json body for a GET request instead of raising an error.

issue_title: patronictl pause does not go into effect inmediately
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce:
- `patronictl pause` on a leader
- restart inmediately the Patroni service on it (i.e, to upgrade Patroni without causing a Postgres failover)

It seems the pause is not applied until the next time the HA loop (10s?) runs, so restarting Patroni before that happens causes Patroni to kill Postgres. The other nodes see the cluster as paused and will not do anything about it.

I haven't found the `paused` state exposed on the API so we can't query it to see if it was applied properly on all nodes - our workaround for now is sleeping some prudential time after it was restarted.

issue_title: Document how to allow long running request not block everything else
issue_body:
Now per default the long running request is blocking all other requests. This is an issue of Flask, but still would make sense to document.

Here is an example from Henning's email:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3                                                                                                                                                                                                                       

import gevent.monkey

gevent.monkey.patch_all()  # CHANGED!

import connexion
import flask
from time import sleep

def get_greeting():

    def generator():
        while True:
            yield "beat\n\n"
            sleep(5)

    response = flask.Response(generator(), mimetype='text/plain', status=200)
    return response

if __name__ == '__main__':

    app.run()
```

issue_title: Z3_goal API breaks invariant I expected to hold
issue_body:
This is a minor issue.

I was playing with Z3's goal API over the weekend and I noticed that an invariant I expected to hold wasn't.

I expected that when I add a formula to a goal (via `Z3_goal_assert()`) the number of formulas in the goal (as reported by `Z3_goal_size()`) to increment by one.

This does not appear to be the case. It seems that the goal eagerly modifies inserted formulas before any tactic is applied to it.

This is what I've observed so far.

* If the inserted formula is a logical `and`  it gets split into separate formula (i.e. the goal size is incremented by `n` for a `n-ary` logical `and`, instead of by 1).
* If `true` is inserted into the goal it is ignored (i.e. increment is 0).
* If `false` is inserted into the goal all existing formulas are removed from the goal and the goal will only contain `false`. Attempting to add more formulas to this goal from now on does nothing.

From the user's perspective I wasn't expecting this to happen. I was expecting Z3 to add the formulas I asked to be inserted into the goal verbatim. I was expecting these changes to be made once a tactic was applied to the goal, not before.

Either the behaviour of `Z3_goal`s needs to be change or it should be documented what `Z3_goal_assert()` will do. My guess is changing the documentation would be the better option.

issue_title: Server handler halts after client connection closed
issue_body:
Pre:
-----
Linux with curl installed.
Python 3.6.3 with aiohttp installed

Server script (server.py):
```
# coding: utf-8

from asyncio import sleep
from aiohttp import web


async def handle_stream(request):
    data = 'Їїїїї'
    resp = web.StreamResponse()
    resp.content_length = len(data)
    await resp.prepare(request)

    resp.write(data.encode("utf-8"))
    await sleep(1)  # if sleep present, handle ends here, next line not executed
    raise RuntimeError("this should happen")


app = web.Application()
app.router.add_get("/", handle_stream)
web.run_app(app)
```

Steps:
--------
1. Run server script: python server.py
2. On the same host run (bash): `curl -vv http://127.0.0.1:8080/`
3. Observe server.py script console output.

Expected:
-------------
3. RuntimeError("this should happen") message 1 second after client disconnects.

Actual:
---------
3. Server runs without messages (raise... line never executed)

_Note:_ If `await sleep...` line (above the `raise...`) removed then exception *does* get triggered.

issue_title: Implement Vehicle.waypoint_home
issue_body:
Vehicle.waypoint_home was in the original documentation, but is not present in the implementation (MPVehicle). According to @geeksville (#100)

Action is to add an implementation for getting this value. Also to consider adding a setter - that makes sense to me because then you could easily change the home position outside of missions.

issue_body:
* Version: 4.0.0-beta.8
* Python: 3.5
* OS: osx


### What was wrong?

I'm trying to deploy a contract by signing the deployment data offline, and then deploying using `web3.eth.sendRawTransaction`. I would like to use this kind of flow when making use of INFURA nodes for my web3 provider.  I am not sure if I am missing something, or if this is even possible. I don't quite understand exactly what `v` represents so I'm at a loss of what to do to fix this issue.

Example code: (eth-tester and py-evm) for the web3 provider.

# 1st default account with eth-tester and py-evm backend

# Provide for web3

# Create contract

# Create deployment data

args = ()
kwargs = {}
arguments = merge_args_and_kwargs(constructor_abi, args, kwargs)

# Create transaction dictionary

    'gasPrice': 2000000000,
    'nonce': web3.eth.getTransactionCount(owner.address),
    'data': deploy_data,
    'chainId': web3.net.version,
    'to': '0x',
}

# Sign
signed_tx = Account.signTransaction(tx, owner.privateKey)

# Sen. Throws and error
tx_hash = web3.eth.sendRawTransaction(signed_tx.rawTransaction)

>> ValidationError: Transaction.v 37 is not less than or equal to 28

### How can it be fixed?
Not quite sure. Thoughts on if this is an issue with my transaction dictionary? Or something else?

issue_title: Can't cast object to dict or Why fill objects data with None?
issue_body:
The new `AbstractObject` initialiser fills the instantiating object data with `None` values for each field.

I could not find an advantage for this nor a drawback for not doing it. Furthermore, since the fields are being taken from `self.Field.__dict__.values()`, which also returns the `__doc__` property with a `None` value, the object's `_data` dict gains a `None` key with a `None` value which prevents casting with the `dict()` function, useful for implicit serialization:

```python
> from facebookads.adobjects.campaign import Campaign
> dict(Campaign())
...
KeyError: 'None'
```

issue_title: MongoDB replica set in MONGODB_URI not supported
issue_body:
In case of a MongoDB replica `,host2[:port2]` is set in MONGODB_URI like it's defined by MongoDB in their [standard URI connection scheme](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/connection-string/#standard-connection-string-format):
```
mongodb://[username:password@]host1[:port1][,host2[:port2],...[,hostN[:portN]]][/[database][?options]]
```

For example:
```
mongodb://username:password@database-host1:12345,database-host2:12345/database?replicaSet=rs-database
```

This is currently not supported by alerta and will result in this stacktrace:
```
[ERROR] Exception in worker process
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gunicorn/arbiter.py", line 557, in spawn_worker
    worker.init_process()
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gunicorn/workers/base.py", line 126, in init_process
    self.load_wsgi()
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gunicorn/workers/base.py", line 136, in load_wsgi
    self.wsgi = self.app.wsgi()
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gunicorn/app/base.py", line 67, in wsgi
    self.callable = self.load()
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gunicorn/app/wsgiapp.py", line 65, in load
    return self.load_wsgiapp()
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gunicorn/app/wsgiapp.py", line 52, in load_wsgiapp
    return util.import_app(self.app_uri)
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gunicorn/util.py", line 357, in import_app
    __import__(module)
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/alerta/app/__init__.py", line 96, in <module>
    db = Mongo()
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/alerta/app/database.py", line 30, in __init__
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/site-packages/alerta/app/database.py", line 48, in connect
    self.connect()
    app.config['MONGO_PORT'] = uri.port
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python2.7/urlparse.py", line 113, in port
    port = int(port, 10)
ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: '12345,database-host2'
```

This is caused by `uri = urlparse(mongo_uri)` in https://github.com/guardian/alerta/blob/master/alerta/app/database.py, because urlparse is not able to parse the scheme with multiple hosts/ports.

Besides this MongoClient is handling this URI perfectly fine.

An alternative would be to use `pymongo.uri_parser`. But, looking a few lines ahead, it's not possible to map multiple hosts to 1 `app.config['MONGO_HOST']` and 1 `app.config['MONGO_PORT']`. Is setting `app.config['XXX']` really necessary there? I've been looking around in the code, but to me it seems those settings are never used after this.

issue_body:
I'm rewriting an old application where tables and columns names are in a foreign language. I would like to translate all to English as soon as possible in the code and limit use of non-english in the code. I also want to fix some inconsistencies in columns names.

First idea was to transform:
```
class User(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    userid = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    user = db.Column(db.String)
```
to
```
class User(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    id = db.Column('userid', db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String)
```
but it raise: `Exception: User has no attribute userid`

Adding `key='id'` has no effect. (`id = db.Column('userid', db.Integer, primary_key=True, key='id')`)

I'll propose a PR for that.

issue_title: Raise TimeoutError on timeout
issue_body:
When I set a timeout like this:

    ser = serial.Serial('/dev/ttyS1', 19200, timeout=1)

pyserial just retries reads like this:

    character = ser.read()

I need to be able to know, when the read has timed out to resend my last message. Please raise a TimeoutError, so I can use pyserial like this:

    while True:
        try:
            character = console.read()
        except TimeoutError:
            console.write(message)
            character = console.read()

I think that it is unpythonic to silently drop errors like a timeout. When a user of pyserial invokes a read(), the expected outcome is that it returns the data that has been read. So hitting the timeout is an error.

issue_title: Admin Integration (a wishlist for someday)
issue_body:
CleanerVersion does not currently integrate with the Django admin, but it may someday. As suggested in #54, here are some features or behaviors that might be considered when integrating CleanerVersion with the Django admin:

 - Create a new version on every edit (or optionally with "create new version" checkbox on edit form)

issue_title: Improving Accuracy and Rejecting Noise mod_pocketsphinx on Freeswitch
issue_body:
We are using mod_pocketsphinx integration with Freeswitch to perform a simple "accept" call feature. Here is the grammar:

=========
#JSGF V1.0;
/**
  * JSGF Grammar for PTA
  */
grammar call_accept;

public <command> = <keyword>;

<keyword> = (accept);
==========

During our testing a set of sample captures (.pcap) we found a very high false positive rate for the following cases:

gibberish.pcap with random words (https://www.dropbox.com/s/68b2ju7ha3i34mg/gibberish.pcap?dl=0)

background_music.pcap with a music played in the background (https://www.dropbox.com/s/h1yzd4arkcwybaw/background_music.pcap?dl=0)

**Note:** Decode the above pcap file as "RTP" on wireshark.

Also, we found that other words such as "okay" and "yes" are wrongly detected as "accept". We see that confidence is 100% in all those cases.

How do we improve this scenario? Should we define an extensive grammar?

We followed: https://sourceforge.net/p/cmusphinx/discussion/help/thread/1c6cb941/?limit=50#efa9 with no luck in mod_pocketsphinx for Freeswitch. 

Any thoughts/suggestion?

https://github.com/enthought/okonomiyaki/blob/master/okonomiyaki/runtimes/runtime_info.py#L111

https://github.com/enthought/okonomiyaki/blob/master/okonomiyaki/runtimes/runtime_info.py#L204

A sample traceback is shown here:
```
2018-03-29 14:25:01 ERROR    1156 Future-thread-1 [edm.utils.misc:154] All Installing runtime attempts failed
2018-03-29 14:25:01 ERROR    1156 Future-thread-1 [canopy.user_env.env_installer:207] Error installing runtime
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "build\bdist.win32\egg\canopy\user_env\env_installer.py", line 205, in do_install

  File "build\bdist.win32\egg\canopy_platform\edm_api.py", line 95, in wrapper
  File "build\bdist.win32\egg\canopy_platform\edm_api.py", line 338, in install_runtime
  File "build\bdist.win32\egg\edm\core\environments_manager.py", line 371, in install
  File "build\bdist.win32\egg\edm\runtimes\runtime_installer.py", line 282, in install
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\Lib\site-packages\okonomiyaki\runtimes\runtime_info.py", line 32, in factory_from_metadata
    return runtime_info_from_metadata(metadata, prefix, name)
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\Lib\site-packages\okonomiyaki\runtimes\runtime_info.py", line 201, in runtime_info_from_metadata
    return klass._from_metadata(metadata, prefix, name)
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\Lib\site-packages\okonomiyaki\runtimes\runtime_info.py", line 100, in _from_metadata
    return cls(*cls._from_metadata_impl(metadata, prefix, name))
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\Lib\site-packages\okonomiyaki\runtimes\runtime_info.py", line 157, in _from_metadata_impl
    metadata, prefix, name
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\Lib\site-packages\okonomiyaki\runtimes\runtime_info.py", line 111, in _from_metadata_impl
    variables = _compute_variables(metadata, prefix, name)
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\Lib\site-packages\okonomiyaki\runtimes\runtime_info.py", line 215, in _compute_variables
    return substitute_variables(variables, variables)
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\Lib\site-packages\okonomiyaki\utils\misc.py", line 89, in substitute_variables
    ret = _resolve(d)
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\Lib\site-packages\okonomiyaki\utils\misc.py", line 86, in _resolve
    ret[k] = substitute_variable(v, local_vars)
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\Lib\site-packages\okonomiyaki\utils\misc.py", line 97, in substitute_variable
    return string.Template(v).substitute(local_vars)
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\lib\string.py", line 176, in substitute
    return self.pattern.sub(convert, self.template)
  File "C:\Program Files\Enthought\Canopy32\App\appdata\canopy-2.1.8.3709.win-x86\lib\string.py", line 166, in convert
    val = mapping[named]
KeyError: u'xxxxxxxx
```
The KeyError is generated when the user name starts with $, for example $xxxxxxxx.

The substitute_variable() function at https://github.com/enthought/okonomiyaki/blob/master/okonomiyaki/utils/misc.py#L96 uses ```string.Template().substitute()``` to perform Shell/Perl style variable substitution. In this case, a user name beginning with $ is treated as an environment variable and an attempt is made to substitute in the value, which then results in a KeyError since the user name is not present as an environment variable.

A simple way to address this would be to use ```string.Template.safe_substitute()```. As shown here: https://gist.github.com/mgrady3/9bf677e4eb84c479095eca477b68f5ae

However, it may be better to instead try and catch KeyErrors and perform some type of check to see where the substitution failed at. Insert some logic to allow paths with directories beginning with '$' to stay, but raise the error if there indeed should have been a substitution but this failed for some other reason.

issue_title: Add django 1.9 support
Confusing documentation for default segment args
issue_body:
After spending quite a bit of time diagnosing why a `hostname` segment in a shell theme wouldn't appear, including carefully checking the [documentation](https://powerline.readthedocs.io/en/master/configuration/segments/common.html#powerline.segments.common.net.hostname) and the [source it links to](https://powerline.readthedocs.io/en/master/_modules/powerline/segments/common/net.html#hostname) (and [on GitHub](/powerline/powerline/blob/develop/powerline/segments/common/net.py#L17)), I finally realized that the `only_in_ssh` argument is set to true in `.../themes/shell/__main__.json`. I had assumed, I think fairly, that optional segment arguments wouldn't be quietly overridden by the default config files.

It would be great if the docs could include a hint that, despite the declaration setting the parameter to `false` by default in the source (and therefore the docs), it is overridden by default for an entire environment. I think I disagree with that override in general, but at the very least I think it would be very helpful to document the full set of defaults for segments in the out-of-the-box config.

If this runs on a remote server, for which I have an entry "server" in my /etc/hosts and I use : 

 `wget -qO- http://server:8000`

It returns the remote server hostname ( as returned by gethostname() )

When using Pulsar's WSGIServer (with --bind 0.0.0.0:8000) it returns 'server', probably because socket.getfqdn('server') fails in server.py and return its argument (and it takes a lot of time to do so because it triggers a DNS lookup, which is problematic in my setup).

I have not taken the time to dive further in the server.py code yet to make a pull request, but I will unless the fix is obvious to you.

Thanks !

issue_title: 按天交易时使用next_bar撮合股票无法隔天买
issue_body:
我的目的是希望实现这样的交易逻辑，但是rqalpha似乎无法做到？
2017-01-04开盘是购入股票600500.XSHG, 然后在2017-01-05开盘时卖出。
我的想法是通过设置撮合方式为next_bar，然后在2017-01-03的handler_bar中购入该股票，然后在2017-01-04时卖出。

但是在实现的时候出现了问题，股票在2017-01-03中买入股票正常，但是在2017-01-04中卖出却提示
`2017-01-04 WARN 订单被拒单: 仓位不足。平仓量: 800，可平数量: 0。`
此时position的状态为
```
print(context.portfolio.positions['600500.XSHG'])
StockPosition({'_order_book_id': '600500.XSHG', '_last_price': nan, '_quantity': 800, '_avg_price': 12.06, '_non_closable': 800, '_frozen': 0, '_transaction_cost': 5})
```
仓位时正常的，应该时系统认为股票时01-04买入，根据当天不能交易的原则所以禁止卖出了。

我觉得如果在撮合方式是next_bar的时候，如果01-03的handle_bar中调用order api买入的股票认为是01-04买入的。那么01-04的handler_bar中调用oder api卖出也应该认为是01-05卖出的吧？
还是我应该使用其他的方式来实现开盘价买入卖出的逻辑呢？

issue_title: ssl_verify on Ubuntu 16.04
issue_body:
Hello,

On Ubuntu 16.04 hen following your instructions to trust our puppet CA given here:

https://pypuppetdb.readthedocs.io/en/v0.1.0/quickstart.html#ssl

and setting `ssl_verify=True` I get no data and an error:

`(Caused by SSLError(SSLError(1, u'[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:661)'),))`

if I omit `ssl_verify` I get the query result and an `InsecureRequestWarning`.  In both cases I have run `update-ca-certificates` and would expect successful verification.  When I run the query with `curl` and don't specify a CA cert I get a successful connection.

Looking at `api.py` it looks like `ssl_verify` is passed to the requests `verify` argument as is:

```
r = self._session.get(url, params=payload,
                                      verify=self.ssl_verify,
                                      cert=(self.ssl_cert, self.ssl_key),
                                      timeout=self.timeout,
                                      auth=auth)
```

and then on the requests docs it looks like verify should be the path to the trusted certificate file or directory.  When I set `ssl_verify='/etc/ssl/certs'` I get the correct query results with no warning.

I'm not sure if this true on other platforms or if it is new, but it seems like you can't trust a custom cert w/o specifying the path currently.


Thanks.

issue_title: DeviceProvidesIncompatibleKey exception when 'not' is given as object attribute
issue_body: For LTM Conditions, 'not' is a valid JSON field, and this is a valid Python keyword. We can get around it (which is what we are doing currently) by doing a setattr and getattr, but this is not sustainable. We must brainstorm a few ideas to convert such things that can be accessible via Python dotted attribute access. (condition._not or condition.invert).

issue_body:
This is per Chris Shenton on the cloudtools-dev group:

```Been using tropo for a few weeks and started using AWACS so I can more easily create IAM Roles and Policy documents. Liking it a lot, thanks, guys.

But I'm stuck trying to reference a resource in an AWACS policy, I can't figure how to build the ARN. The docs show hard-wired names, e.g.:

S3:  Resource=[s3.S3_ARN("myBucket")],
SQS: Resource=[sqs.SQS_ARN(region, account, "queue1"), ],

For things that need Region and Account, is there a way to get those without hard-wiring them in my tropo script?  I was hoping to use Ref("AWS::Region") and Ref("AWS::AccountID") but those generate resources like:

"Resource": [
   "arn:aws:dynamodb:<troposphere.Ref object at 0x10978c128>:<troposphere.Ref object at 0x10978c1d0>:table/JobStateDB"
],

I'd also like to avoid hard-wiring my resource name; for example, I have a DynamoDB defined like:

r_jobstate_db = t.add_resource(
    dynamodb.Table(
        "JobStateDB",
        AttributeDefinitions=[dynamodb.AttributeDefinition("job", "S")],
        KeySchema=[dynamodb.Key("job", "HASH")],
        ProvisionedThroughput=dynamodb.ProvisionedThroughput(
            Ref(p_jobstate_readunits),
            Ref(p_jobstate_writeunits),
        )
    ))

And I want to reference it in AWACS. Below, I know the Ref()s fail as above; I think I'm relying on knowing the implementation when I access the ".title" attribute:

                    Statement(
                        Action=[awacs.dynamodb.PutItem, ],
                        Effect=Allow,
                        Resource=[
                            awacs.dynamodb.ARN(
                                Ref("AWS::Region"),
                                Ref("AWS::AccountID"),
                                r_jobstate_db.title,
                                ),
                            ],

Any guidance on how I can:
* Get AWS "Region" and "AccountID" to build the ARN without hard-wiring
* Get the resources name to build the ARN without peeking at code

Or is there a totally smarter way to do this, like:

  Resource=[awacs.dyamodb.MagickARN(r_jobstate_db)]

Thanks!
```

This seems like something we should investigate.  Not sure if this is:

1. Possible in regular cloudformation iam policies.
2. A problem with awacs

Anyway, I think it's curious enough that we should have a bug for it just to make sure it doesn't get lost.

issue_title: Make column keys for import language neutral
as_future and generators
issue_body:
Both twisted and asyncio come with decorators which allow to write async code as if synchronous (`@inlineCallbacks` and `@coroutine` for reference). However it's easy to forget these decorators either while writing code or while refactoring code (e.g switching from a synchronous implementation of a func to async).

I argue that in 90% of all cases where a function returning a generator is passed into `as_future` the user actually just forgot the decorator. This can lead to nasty and hard to debug behaviour. E.g Autobahn's `ApplicationSession.onJoin` handler will simply not be invoked at all, but at the same time there isn't any feedback about this as well. The application will just continue as usual although code which was intended to be executed, was not. Due to the dynamic nature of Python, this can mean that actual errors might happen much much later. Cause and effect might not be that obvious to identify due to them being seperated by a very long period of time.

I propose that returning a generator from a function which is passed into `as_future` is to be considered an error. For the case that one actually wants to return a generator, the function should be decorated with `@txaio.returns_gen` which will wrap the generator in a `_Generator` marker object telling the `as_future` implementation, that returning a generator was actually intented.

I'm aware of the fact that this is not consistent in behaviour with e.g `maybeDeferred` but I believe this the right place to implement that check. Otherwise it would mean that every piece of code which would need such a check would have to do it on its own which is just as easy to forget. Also this adds alot of boilerplate everytime.

issue_title: Protocol Error: PUBLISHED received for non-pending request ID
issue_body:
I am using a [wampcc router](https://github.com/darrenjs/wampcc), with Autobahn|Python clients. They work completely fine, until I try to publish a message from one of the Python clients.

I have the following setup:
1 WAMPCC router
1 Autobahn|Python publish client
1 Autobahn|Python subscribe client

You can also find the code of my minimal setup here: https://gist.github.com/Mdrbhatti/6926a1c9bfba780573c92bea1982ddec

This is the error I get when I try to run publish.py

```sh
publish: com.myapp.topic1 0
2018-08-07T10:54:26 Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Python37\lib\site-packages\autobahn\wamp\websocket.py", line 95, in onMessage
    self._session.onMessage(msg)
  File "C:\Python37\lib\site-packages\autobahn\wamp\protocol.py", line 701, in onMessage
    raise ProtocolError("PUBLISHED received for non-pending request ID {0}".format(msg.request))
autobahn.wamp.exception.ProtocolError: PUBLISHED received for non-pending request ID 1

2018-08-07T10:54:26 session closed with reason wamp.close.transport_lost [WAMP transport was lost without closing the session before]
publish: com.myapp.topic1 1
2018-08-07T10:54:27 Task exception was never retrieved
future: <Task finished coro=<Component.onJoin() done, defined at publish.py:38> exception=TransportLost()>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "publish.py", line 42, in onJoin
    self.publish(u'com.myapp.topic1', counter)
  File "C:\Python37\lib\site-packages\autobahn\wamp\protocol.py", line 1289, in publish
    raise exception.TransportLost()
autobahn.wamp.exception.TransportLost
```

Currently. the wampcc router is sending a PUBLISHED ack message for every PUBLISH it receives. I cannot find anything in the [wamp-proto RFC](https://wamp-proto.org/static/rfc/draft-oberstet-hybi-crossbar-wamp.html#rfc.section.9.2.2) that requires the session to be closed on receiving an unwanted PUBLISHED message. For more context: darrenjs/wampcc#40

I have also tried AutobahnC++ with a wampcc router, and I had no errors or issues with publishing/subscribing to it. I was able to fix this error by passing the options parameter in the publish method:

```python
self.publish(u'com.myapp.topic1', counter, options=PublishOptions(acknowledge=True))
```

issue_title: Bug: YAML serialization of git sha1
issue_body:
In the event that the git short hash of a repository is something like `01234567`, then the image tag is serialized unquoted, resulting in a broken `values.yaml`.

This is a bit hard to reproduce, as it would happen on only ~0.3% of the commits.

You can reproduce the issue by running:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/SwissDataScienceCenter/renku-ui
cd renku-ui
git checkout -b test 0192117
cd helm-chart
chartpress
```

Then, you can check the `values.yaml` file contents:
```bash
cat renku-ui/values.yaml
```
```yaml
[...]
image:
  name: renku/renku-ui
  repository: renku/renku-ui
  tag: 0192117
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
[...]
```

As you can see, this will result in `image.tag` equals to `192117` and not `0192117`.

A potential fix would be to always quote sha1 tags.

issue_title: encoding of \u2028 and \u2029 is incorrect
issue_body:
the [spec](https://matrix.org/docs/spec/appendices.html#canonical-json) says that `\u2028` (line separator) should be encoded as the UTF-8 `\xe2\x80\xa8`, however python-canonicaljson encodes it as the `\u`-escaped version.

This is largely thanks to https://github.com/simplejson/simplejson/issues/206.

issue_title: Invalid Border Agent Locator in leader network data (unable to join)
issue_body:
I found that leader + commissioner can eventually go to an invalid state where Commissioner is sending keep alives and it is accepted by the leader but nobody can join.

The problem shows up when a border agent sent a leader petition in a child state (not leader, nor router). In that case a child RLOC is saved in network data Border Agent Locator.
When this thread device changes it's state to "router" its RLOC is changed but no-one updates this field in leader network data.

From this point a border agent sends LEAD_KA to the leader with commissioner session ID and gets valid response (Accept) but the border agent locator in the network data points to the device that doesn't exist.

I reproduced the issue using 3 ot_cli_ftd.
1. Run two clis and make sure that one is a leader and one is a child.
2. On the child cli make commissioner start
3. Make sure that it is still a child and verify netdatashow - this device RLOC should be found in network data. At this point any device can join as commissioner RLOC is the same as the one in network data.
4. Wait until commissioner changes state to router or force it by "state router"
5. Network data doesn't change and no other device can successfully join - never ...

I couldn't find any related text in Thread docs but I think that leader could check border agent locator when he receives LEAD_KA.

issue_title: Responses to MLE requests sent by SED
issue_body:
When I experimented with a SED device I noticed a small problem:
1. SED attaches to a parent.
2. Parent sends Data Response to the SED (new Pending Dataset).
3. SED sends Data Request to receive new Pending Dataset.
4. Parent puts Data Response with the new Dataset to the indirect queue.
5. SED is going to retrieve Data Response when poll timer expires. It can be in a few hours or days.

Maybe we could provide a procedure to notify MAC layer that MLE (or any other protocol) expects response. During waiting for a response the poll period could be much shorter than one set by an application.

This could be somehow a solution to #1353

issue_title: Locating types in public includes?
issue_body:
At the start of the OpenThread project, there were only two public include files: `openthread-types.h` and `openthread.h`.  As features were added to the OpenThread API, the set of public include files grew beyond those two files.  Most recently, #1173 further broke out APIs into feature-specific include files, based on feedback in #1100.

The current state of OpenThread is that we continue to have an [openthread/types.h](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/master/include/openthread/types.h) that includes a large number of types.  At the same time, we have other public includes that also include types (e.g. [openthread/instance.h](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/master/include/openthread/instance.h), and [openthread/udp.h](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/master/include/openthread/udp.h)).

It seems we should be consistent about where to place types.  I see a few options moving forward:

1. Colocate all OpenThread types into a single [openthread/types.h](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/master/include/openthread/types.h).
2. Break out types into their feature-specific headers.
3. A hybrid, where types for core features (those always compiled in) are included in something like a `openthread/core-types.h` and break out optional features (those with configure options) into feature-specific headers.

This issue is to collect thoughts and feedback from the OpenThread community.

issue_title: Out of band test selection fails old and new python test runs
issue_body:
Both old and new python backends support passing args to `py.test` directly. If `-k` is used to sub-select tests to run from the given target set, a run can fail if:
1. All tests are de-selected (`-k` intersects no tests in the target set).
2. All tests for a single target are de-selected and the test run mode is `--no-fast`.

This behavior contradicts our own internal short-circuiting of `py.test` executions with syntesized `0` exit codes when we detect no test targets or no test sources up-front.

issue_title: Pantsd doesn't properly close its output in some way
issue_body:
The following works fine and exits:
```
cat <(./pants list 3rdparty:: >/dev/null)
```

The following hangs forever, never closing the fd:
```
cat <(./pants --enable-pantsd=True list 3rdparty:: >/dev/null)
```

This makes it hard to use pants's more query-ish goals (like `dependencies` and `dependees`) in scripts.

issue_title: GoBuildgen is lossy when materializing indirect remote dep BUILDs
issue_body:
Unless those indirect remote deps are included in the initial build graph via, for example:
```
./pants buildgen.go --materialize --remote 3rdparty/golang::
```
Any rev specified in a pre-existing BUILD of an indirect (transitive) remote import path dep is lost.

This is related to #2372 which was only partially fixed as it turns out!

issue_title: Improve design and ux of documentation site
Remove dead code from wire_gen.py
issue_body:
In wire, remove support for --service-factory, it only works with a few versions of 2.0.0BETA.
The wire project has changed the way it generates code in wire 2.0 and the --service-factory interface was short lived.  The 2.0 service hasn't settled down yet and is still pre GA.

At this point, it would be sufficient to remove --service-factory from wire 2 support, and leave code in place to give an error if --service-writer is used with a version of wire > 1.x

issue_title: New pre-commit hooks for Java, Kotlin, Golang and YAML formatting
issue_body:
Hello maintainers,
I'm currently working on some projects Java/Kotlin based and I'd love to use pre-commit to ensure that the code respects a pre-determined code style, more or less what pep8 and autopep8-wrapper pre-commit hooks are doing for python.

I did create a temporary repository to test the potential new pre-commit hooks and I was wondering if it could make sense to integrate them here or if you rather prefer having a separate repository for it.

The current WIP repo is available on http://github.com/macisamuele/maci-pre-commit-hooks.

issue_title: search method should support start_index and max_results
issue_body:
# Issue
CROWD API search results that exceed the server's maximum result set size are not accessible through the python-crowd.py project. The current search method supports CROWD API query parameter of entity_type for the /usermanagement/1/search resource, but has not implemented support for start_index or max_results parameters. 

# Solution
I propose to enhance the search method to implement two additional query parameters which the CROWD API supports: start_index and max_results. Implementation should include default values of start_index=0 and max_results=99999 to maintain backwards compatibility with existing tools not currently supplying these arguments when calling the search method.

# Benefits
Supporting start_index and max_results query parameters will…

- Allow retrieval of results exceeding the CROWD server's maximum results set size.
- Allow performance tuning if multiple, smaller query results would be better for server or client performance.

# Potential Issues
Existing tools using the python-crowd module that rely upon a server's default max_results setting may encounter a change in behavior as the query results could be larger (or smaller) than anticipated due to the proposed default value of 99999. According to the Atlassian REST API policy, the default and maximum sizes of paged data are not considered part of the API and may change without notice. Tools using the CROWD API should declare and validate results set sizes themselves. [https://developer.atlassian.com/platform/marketplace/atlassian-rest-api-policy/#AtlassianRESTAPIpolicy-Resource/ratelimitsandpaging](https://developer.atlassian.com/platform/marketplace/atlassian-rest-api-policy/#AtlassianRESTAPIpolicy-Resource/ratelimitsandpaging)

issue_title: node.slaveoutput has no key 'environment' when used with xdist and cygwin
issue_body:
I know, this is a horrendous configuration; it's what we're working with.

On line 73 of `pytest_html/plugin.py` the key `environment` is not present in`node.slaveoutput`. As the title says, this condition is reached when executing pytest with xdist over multiple ssh hosts, that themselves are running ssh daemons with cygwin. I've been able to get around this using:

```
 73         if 'environment' not in node.slaveoutput:
 74             node.slaveoutput['environment'] = None
```

But I am this certain causes issues in other circumstances, e.g. when trying to access the `environment` attribute when generating reports. I could instead simply add 'Not Found'... Would you accept a PR with this? I can also give the xdist guys a heads up.

issue_title: ENH: Provide access to returns tables in top-level API
issue_body: Accessing the internal functions to get the tables of returns provides returns that are unscaled and misleading, something that is handled and abstracted away in the top-level API of Alphalens with the tear sheets. issue_title: URLType regex doesn't match urls that have subdomains with underscores
issue_body:
If you use the URL_REGEX value from the URLType, it doesn't match the following valid url: `http://my_subdomain.mydomain.com/` If you remove the underscore, it works. 

It seems like the best thing here might be to use another project's url regex. Django, for example, has one that's well-tested and does work on the above url: https://github.com/django/django/blob/f8cc464452f495fce2a3d6f7494396c8f798a1e6/django/core/validators.py#L67

issue_title: Bold font may not be activated on new terminals
issue_body:
According to [xterm.js#157](https://github.com/sourcelair/xterm.js/issues/157), bold fonts are disabled if the ``isBoldBroken`` function determines if the current font has no bold variant. Right now it seems that our fallback font, ``ubuntu-powerline`` is not loading its bold variant, therefore, bold fonts are disabled by default, even if the font is changed via Qt.

We should bundle plain Ubuntu Mono instead, this font actually works and enables the bold variants.

issue_title: Universal sentence encoder speed
issue_body:
Hi, 

I am trying the sample code provided with tensorflow hub. I want to use the sentence embedding vectors at runtime. It is consistently taking about 4 seconds on CPU for one sentence. Is there something i can do to speed it up ?   

```
import tensorflow as tf
import tensorflow_hub as hub
import time

# Import the Universal Sentence Encoder's TF Hub module
embed = hub.Module("https://tfhub.dev/google/universal-sentence-encoder/1")

sentence = "I am a sentence for which I would like to get its embedding."
messages = [sentence]

with tf.Session() as session:
  session.run([tf.global_variables_initializer(), tf.tables_initializer()])
  t1 = time.time()
  message_embeddings = session.run(embed(messages))
  print time.time() - t1

```

issue_title: ParseError message does not provide enough insight
issue_labels: [{'id': 429586299, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/area:%20code', 'name': 'area: code', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 429588767, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/resolution:%20fixed', 'name': 'resolution: fixed', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}, {'id': 429587821, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/type:%20feature', 'name': 'type: feature', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### Actual behavior

The message text of `zhmcclient.ParseError` exceptions is pretty thin and does not provide sufficient context. for example:

```
ParseError: Parse error in returned JSON: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)
```

### Expected behavior

In this example, the response content seems to be empty, but the particular function in the zhmcclient code that raises this `ParseError` should be called only for status codes that indicate non-empty content. Therefore, interesting additional information would be the HTTP method and URL, and the HTTP status code.

### Execution environment

* zhmcclient version: 2.13.0
* Operating system (type+version): RHEL 7.3
* HMC version: 2.13.1
* CPC version: 2.13.1

issue_title: Spark token: read from env
issue_body:
It is a good practice not to embedd secretes in code.
Cloud practices (see 12factor.net) are to read from an env variable.
I suggest we read by default from SPARK_TOKEN env variable.

Moreover, any sample we provide will leverage the env variable approach.

issue_title: rename utility classes pull-left/pull-right
issue_body:
the utility classes should be renamed into the fa-namespace:

* `pull-left` » `fa-pull_left`
* `pull-right` » `fa-pull_right`

These are common names for floating behavior, and user (or 3rd party) css sheets may define styles other than just a float -- which could conflict with fa styles.

i suggest using an underscore for utility classes, because the dashes are used for icons.

issue_title: Python 2.6 is being abandoned
issue_body:
Python 2.6 support is being dropped from openstack. As of Juno, openstack requires python 2.7. We're still trying to support Icehouse, which could optionally run with 2.6.

The first problem we've encountered is a dictionary comprehension is oslo messaging, which causes our `py26` tests to fail. Oslo messaging is no longer tested against python 2.6. https://github.com/openstack/oslo.messaging/commit/2734fc8f505ca07ff1a4f4a420807e5c1b4ed812

We need to set upper bounds on the `requirements.txt` and `testrequirements.txt` for python 2.6. We can change the requirements for `py26` tests in `tox.ini`. I don't know how we can change the requirements for the library for python 2.6,

issue_title: run_transaction uses bad savepoint name with explicit commit()
issue_body:
Running the following yields `psycopg2.InternalError: SAVEPOINT not supported except for COCKROACH_RESTART`

I believe this is due to https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroachdb-python/blob/master/cockroachdb/sqlalchemy/transaction.py#L54.

Why do we need `savepoint_state.cockroach_restart`? Considering that CockroachDB supports no other savepoint name, why not hard-code it and drop the conditional in the following functions:
https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroachdb-python/blob/master/cockroachdb/sqlalchemy/dialect.py#L158 

Steps to reproduce:
```
docker run -p 26257:26257 cockroachdb/cockroach:v1.0 start --insecure
```
Then run the following code:
https://gist.github.com/gpaul/648189354975992a2c285143d08be88a

This gives the following output using `cockroachdb-python v0.1`:
https://gist.github.com/gpaul/14fa2967c42d4ccf6b223322a7967476

issue_title: How to handle cert transfers from one account to another? How to administer existing accounts as 3rd party?
issue_body:
There seem to be some open questions to us as a webhosting provider regarding implementation of Let's Encrypt as a service for our customers:

How is it possible to transfer a Let's Encrypt certificate from one account to another?

As a hosting provider we'd like to be able to install new LE certs or transfer existing LE certs installed on another hosting provider via some sort of administrative API, how would we be able to handle that, given the restriction that every account only has one keypair? Of course, our users would have to grant us the permissions to do so, but this kind of third party involvement does not seem to be fleshed out anywhere in the current ACME specs nor the boulder implementation, is that correct? How would we go about that?

How would we automatically import a LE cert from another hosting provider for a given user?

issue_title: Installer plugin documentation is lacking/confusing
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm trying to write an installer plugin. The IInstaller interface has lots of different functions that I seemingly need to override, but the documentation is very unclear about the larger picture. In normal use, I can only see prepare, deploy_cert, save and restart be called; however, there are lots of others, including get_all_names (which is hard for me to find without opening and parsing the actual certificates) and then lots of rollback/recovery stuff which somehow may or may not integrate with a “reverter” module. I _think_ some of the rollback stuff is called if I throw an exception from any of the others, but exactly when a checkpoint is set (I can be asked to rollback N checkpoints, so it would be good to know when I am expected to take a checkpoint) or how all of this is used is unclear.

It looks like somehow the installer interface might also be used for authentication via DVSNI. If that's a correct guess (so it means installers overlap with authenticators wrt. purpose), perhaps it should be split out from the much simpler concept of just deploying an existing certificate authenticated by another module?

Thanks!

issue_title: Cert file location for domains with multiple SANs
issue_body:
Very minor issue, but when I ran `letsencrypt certonly` with domains like

```ini
domains = example.com, www.example.com, a.example.com, b.example.com, c.example.com
```

I expected that the cert files would go into `/etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com`, but to my surprise they actually went into `/etc/letsencrypt/live/a.example.com`.

For a better location, I'd suggest either using the first domain name specified, or the root domain if all domains are subdomains.

issue_title: Plan when to end support for Python 2.7 and 3.4
issue_body:
Supporting multiple old versions of Python is becoming more and more burdensome as good features get added to newer versions of Python.

I don't want to abandon support for versions of Python that users are actively using.  But I also don't want to waste developer time supporting versions nobody cares about.

So whoever reads this, please feel free to add a comment and say what versions of Python you care about ``cmd2`` supporting now and in the near future.

issue_title: [WebUi] Task Timeline Doesn't Appear in Firefox
issue_body:
If you launch the webui in Firefox and attempt to generate the task timeline, only a large blank area is shown. This occurs with Firefox 56.
Steps to reproduce:
Execute the following:
```
import ray
ray.init()
@ray.remote
def f():
  return 1
f.remote()
```
and go into the webui and attempt to generate the task timeline.

The issue appears to be that Firefox does not support the WebComponents that Catapult needs. There are two files `index.html` and `trace-viewer-full.html` that the task timeline is composed of. `index.html` imports `trace-viewer-full.html` via HTMLImports, which is [not supported](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Web_Components/HTML_Imports) by Firefox. Even if the content of `trace-viewer-full.html` is put into `index.html`, then you still need to enable webcomponents in `about:config` in firefox. If enabled, then some function calls still do not work as in [this issue](https://github.com/catapult-project/catapult/issues/2489).

I'm not certain if we can do anything to fix this in Ray beyond potentially including the right version of webcomponents.

issue_title: Jenkins test failure "Failed to decode stderr_data".
issue_body:
The Jenkins tests occasionally fail with the error

```python
raise Exception("Failed to decode stderr_data:", stderr_data)
```

This is an exception we added in in #567 in order to print more information when this error occurred.

From the logs, it looks like it's failing because of the driver ID in the stderr. This should be fixable if we print the driver ID in hex instead, although there may be a better solution.

See these logs for an example. https://amplab.cs.berkeley.edu/jenkins/job/Ray-PRB/910/console

issue_title: 12 am/pm
issue_body: According to [this wiki page](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/12-hour_clock#Confusion_at_noon_and_midnight) noon/midnight could be written in different ways. We should check if we parsing `12 noon`/`noon` correctly and also add an option to choose how we should treat 12 am/pm.

issue_title: Multiple minor issues the the walkthru
issue_body:
On this page: http://soft-matter.github.io/trackpy/tutorial/walkthrough.html

1. The first line fails under ipython and python and on various versions (2.7 and 3.3):

%matplotlib inline

2. The "print" command also fails on py 3.3:

print frames[0]  # the first frame

as it require parentheses:

print (frames[0])  # the first frame

3. The print command also fails elsewhere on the page:

print 'Before:', t['particle'].nunique()
print 'After:', t1['particle'].nunique()

4. The "frames[0]" command shows a plot in the documentation but it doesn't work in reality (elsewhere the plots seem to display fine as per the documentation).

5. Py 2.7 does not appear to like this line:

frames = pims.ImageSequence('./sample_data/bulk_water/*.png', as_grey=True)

It complains about converting to greyscale.

6. I think it should be made explicitly clear in the documentation what python version the devs are using.  I have spent considerable time trying to use trackpy with py 3.4 which I could not make work.

issue_title: Flipping over of video frame and trajectory plot
issue_body: I think it would be useful if the orientation of the video frames and the trajectory plots was the same.  At the moment on the walkthrough code that I have been using the plots can be confusing due to the flipping over of the trajectory plot wrt the video frame.  Also the trajectory plot is x and y trimmed to fit the tracked particles rather than left with the same dimensions as the video file.  This is especially noticeable when the video frame has a few particles near the middle of the frame.  In this case the two representations are barely recognisable, in my case at least.

issue_title: Add support for Fashion-MNIST dataset
issue_body:
[fashion-mnist: A MNIST-like fashion product database. Benchmark](https://github.com/zalandoresearch/fashion-mnist)
Here is the reasons:
* **MNIST is too easy.** Convolutional nets can achieve 99.7% on MNIST. Classic machine learning algorithms can also achieve 97% easily. 
* **MNIST is overused.** In this April 2017 Twitter thread, Google Brain research scientist and deep learning expert Ian Goodfellow calls for people to move away from MNIST.
* **MNIST can not represent modern CV tasks**, as noted in this April 2017 Twitter thread, deep learning expert/Keras author François Chollet.
* **Many ML libraries already include Fashion-MNIST data/API**, such as, PyTorch, Torch, MXNet etc.
* **It shares the same image size and structure of training and testing splits with MNIST**

issue_title: csvgrep does not return results if more than one column is specified
issue_body:
Say you use a csv file `tips.csv` (test file from the `pandas` package) which starts like

    total_bill,tip,sex,smoker,day,time,size
    16.99,1.01,Female,No,Sun,Dinner,2
    10.34,1.66,Male,No,Sun,Dinner,3
    21.01,3.5,Male,No,Sun,Dinner,3
    23.68,3.31,Male,No,Sun,Dinner,2
    24.59,3.61,Female,No,Sun,Dinner,4
    25.29,4.71,Male,No,Sun,Dinner,4

With this if you issue on the command line

   $ csvgrep -c tip -m 3.31 tips.csv 

you get as expected 

    total_bill,tip,sex,smoker,day,time,size
    23.68,3.31,Male,No,Sun,Dinner,2

but if you search through more columns,e.g.:

   $ csvgrep -c tip,sex -m 3.31 tips.csv 

you get  no data row back:

    total_bill,tip,sex,smoker,day,time,size

This is wrong. csvgrep should return the same data row
`23.68,3.31,Male,No,Sun,Dinner,2`. The same happens when specifying
the columns by index (`-c 2` works, but e.g. `-c 1,2` does not).

Additionally it would be great to have an easy way to tell csvgrep to search all columns.

<!--  LocalWords:  csvgrep csv
 -->

@Jeltje reports in https://github.com/BD2KGenomics/toil-scripts/issues/447 that the workflow directories are not being cleared up /var/lib/toil/toil-UUID. In a subsequent run, I've also seen on one of her workers that a previous workflow's directory was left on disk, a workflow that was terminated via Ctrl-C on the leader. It is possible the left overs from https://github.com/BD2KGenomics/toil-scripts/issues/447 were also caused by termination via Ctrl-C but that's not a reliable assumption.

issue_title: Use of ntpath instead of os.path?
issue_body:
The `ntpath` module is specifically for manipulating Windows-format paths on any platform, while `os.path` works with paths of the platform you are currently on.

How come you're using `ntpath` in e.g. https://github.com/BD2KGenomics/toil-vg/blob/master/src/toil_vg/vg_evaluation_pipeline.py#L186?

issue_title: UnicodeCSVWriter raises AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'writerows'
issue_body:
when it used in a second variant in the `with` statement and a filename has passed to the constructor. I've tried smth like: 
from oscar.core.compat import UnicodeCSVWriter

data = [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]]
with UnicodeCSVWriter('test.csv') as writer:
    writer.writerows(data)`
and have got AttributeError, while `test.csv` file was created but remains empty. However, it works perfectly in the first variant: `writer = UnicodeCSVWriter(open_file=fhandler)` It seems like `return self` should be in the end of the `__enter__` method (here: https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/master/src/oscar/core/compat.py#L154 )

issue_title: inter-thread force_locale interference
issue_body:
There seems to be a problem with Flask-Babel==0.11.1 in multithreaded Flask app.
It can be revealed (even in threaded werkzeug, but first encountered in mod_wsgi env) by adding debug point to https://github.com/python-babel/flask-babel/blob/master/flask_babel/__init__.py#L337 (just after "temporarily" changing babel's `locale_selector_func`). Then application, accessed from another thread, is stuck with `lambda: locale`, whatever locale happened to be.

If it's not easy to fix, it should be properly indicated, that context manager in question is not thread-safe.

Flask is 0.10.1.

(also:
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/43470984/flask-babel-not-thread-safe
)

issue_title: Infinite loop in exposure.equalize_adapthist()
issue_body:
The `equalize_adapthist()` function can hang, caused by the following:

When using a very small clip_limit parameter (e.g. 0.001) on a grayscale image with low contrast, the function `clip_histogram()` in `_adapthist.py` hangs in an infinite loop because the `n_excess` variable is not being lowered in the `while n_excess > 0` loop.

issue_title: skimage.io.imread returns incorrect dimensions (according to docs)
issue_body:
See here: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/30088841

Importing a TIFF image using skimage.io.imread returns an array with shape (3,M,N) instead of (M,N,3). This breaks the flatten and as_grey features. The results are the same calling `imread` with `plugin='tifffile'`.

    In [1]: im = skimage.io.imread('image.tif')

    In [2]: im.shape
    Out[2]: (3, 768, 1024)

    In [3]: im = skimage.io.imread('image.tif', as_grey = True)

    In [4]: im.shape
    Out[4]: (3, 768)

    In [5]: im = skimage.io.imread('image.tif', flatten = True)
 
    In [6]: im.shape
    Out[6]: (3, 768)

issue_title: Problems with multiprocessing caused by the zipfile module
issue_body:
I was investigating on a really strange issue today: I was using `fmpy` in our project together with a larger multiprocessing setup (doing parallel simulations) and I encountered a strange error (which was hard to debug due to multiprocess stuff) when running on Linux (funnily, Windows worked). The result was a `BrokenProcessPool` error or a `queue.Full` error. 
After several hours of slicing away code line by code line, I ended up with the following script:

```python
def use_fmpy(*args):
    import fmpy
    return fmpy.read_model_description('bouncingBall_me.fmu') 

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import tables  ##### TRIGGER HERE

    import concurrent.futures as cf
    with cf.ProcessPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) as pool:
        futures = []
        for i in range(10):
            future = pool.submit(use_fmpy, None)
            futures.append(future)
        cf.wait(futures)
```

What made it even harder was the fact that the `import tables` line triggered the error in the multiprocessing subprocesses. With the pytables import I got the error, without pytables, the error wasn't appearing.

So I first suspected pytables for being the source of the problem, but after a while I took a closer look at the `read_model_description` function. (BTW I tried out all available fmpy versions) The first lines of this function look like this:

https://github.com/CATIA-Systems/FMPy/blob/35245dd8bf2b8be2a8e750b04bd29df4557d3032/fmpy/model_description.py#L210-L212

It uses the `zipfile` module to read the `modelDescription.xml` file from the FMU file. Putting a `return` just before this code block made my example above work in all cases, so it must have had to do with the `zipfile` use.

Googling brought immediately a possible issue: [It seems that the `zipfile` module is *not threadsafe*](https://stackoverflow.com/a/22853969/195476). So my idea was to replace the `zipfile` use by a call to `unzip`:

```python
import subprocess
root = etree.fromstring(subprocess.check_output(
    ['unzip', '-p', filename, 'modelDescription.xml'])) 
```

And surprise: it worked! No more problems with `BrokenProcessPool` or `queue.Full` errors.

So my question here: would it be possible that you replace all the `zipfile` usages by something more robust? I would really love to use `fmpy` instead of `pyfmi`, but this issue cost me already 5 hours of debugging and it is currently a blocker for us.
